articles.title: Fuel prices March 26-April 1: RON97, unsubsidised RON95 up 60sen, diesel in peninsula up 80sen
articles.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The retail price of diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will rise by 80sen to RM5.52 per litre, while prices in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan will remain unchanged at RM2.15 per litre from March 2

articles.title: Only permit holders can buy petrol and diesel using drums, containers, says Domestic Trade Ministry
articles.summary: PETALING JAYA: Only those with special permits are allowed to purchase petrol or diesel in containers at petrol stations, says the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry. Read full story

articles.title: 'Sunyi Dalam Seragam' poem strikes chord with audience on 219th Police Day event
articles.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 219th Police Day celebration on Wednesday (March 25) fell into a rare hush as a poetry recital laid bare the quiet sacrifices of those in uniform. Read full story

articles.title: Probe papers on top DOE officials submitted to AGC, prosecution recommended, says MACC
articles.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has submitted investigation papers involving two senior Environment Department (DOE) officials to the Attorney General's Chambers (AGC), r

articles.title: Take proactive steps as prolonged dry spell could be on horizon, Warisan veep urges Sabah govt
articles.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The state government has been urged to take proactive measures amid scientific projections that Sabah could face a prolonged dry spell induced by the El Niño phenomenon. Read full story

articles.title: 14 Malaysian universities break into QS World Top 50
articles.summary: PETALING JAYA: A total of 14 Malaysian universities now rank in the global Top 50 of the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026. Read full story

articles.title: Pengerang forest fire: 120ha controlled, 30ha still burning
articles.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Efforts to contain a secondary forest fire behind the Pengerang People's Housing Project (PPR), which began on Tuesday afternoon (March 24), are still ongoing. Read full story

articles.title: No one should interfere in police's duty to administer justice, says Anwar
articles.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: No one should interfere in the police's duty in administering justice, especially against large scale crime syndicates, says the Prime Minister. Read full story
